title: Vivi Sees Her Country’s Pain
description: On pages 13–16, this part focuses on vivi sees her country’s pain, showing the key character choices and conflict behind this chapter beat.
scene: Vivi Sees Her Country’s Pain. The Straw Hats reach Alabasta and immediately see the public admiration for Crocodile, the Warlord behind the kingdom’s crisis. The scene description focuses on what the reader needs to understand before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Alabasta citizen
dialogue.text: Well I guess that's to be expected... but to think the so-called hero of Alabasta,

dialogue.speaker: Alabasta citizen
dialogue.text: zzz...

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: would be secretly planning on taking over the country...

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: I don't think anybody would've imagined that even in their dreams.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Either way, all I have to do,

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: Is Kick That Guy's Ass, Right!?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Yes... If we can put a stop to the civil unrest first,

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: And chase away Baroque works, then alabaster will be saved...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Oh, about that.

dialogue.speaker: Alabasta citizen
dialogue.text: Exactly how is this Baroque Works. Organization set up again?

dialogue.speaker: Alabasta citizen
dialogue.text: And what's with the "MR" and "Miss" business?

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: BAROGUE WORKS...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: It's quite simple, really.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: At the top, you have crocodile, who goes by the codename Mr. O...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Then you have the 12 people and 1 animal who are his "Agents" that receive their orders directly from Mr. O.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: These 13 agents are all named Mr. followed by a number signifying their rank.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: And each of these agents is paired with a female agent who's similar to them in terms of strength.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 1
dialogue.text: Mr. 13 and his partner miss Friday are a special case since they serve as the executioners that punish those who fail their missions.

dialogue.speaker: Alabasta citizen
dialogue.text: And among the agents, those who are either rank 5 or above are collectively called Officer agents.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Most of these officer agents are devil fruit users,

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: Ms. Valentine

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: And they only carry out the most important missions.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: Ms. Mother's Day Ms. Father's Day Ms. Monday

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Ms. Wednesday, Ms. Tuesday Ms. Thursday Ms. Saturday Ms. Saturday Ms. Saturday Ms. Saturday Ms. Saturday Ms. Saturday Ms. Saturday Ms. Saturday Ms. Saturday

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Below them in rank are the frontier agents.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 1
dialogue.text: Mr. 6 Mr. 7 Mr. 8 Mr. 9 Mr. 10 Mr.11 Mr.112

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: They act as the leaders of unranked followers called "Millions,"

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: To collect reward money for hunting down pirates at the entrance of Grand Line.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: This is the basic rundown of the Baroque works organization.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Oh, I get it! So all I gotta do,

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Is Kick Crocodile's Ass, Right!?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: You didn't understand a thing, did ya?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: So assuming that,

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: Taking over Alabasta is Baroque Works' final objective...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Then those ones you call,

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: "Officer Agents"...

summary: This part highlights vivi sees her country’s pain and moves the chapter forward through its central emotional or plot turn.
